name: The Twelve Houses — a chart-wheel casting
description: The chart-shaped casting: the twelve houses as twelve fixed positions on the wheel — the richer target the three-position casting graduates into. Nothing is drawn at random: the positions ARE your chart. Each house resolves to the sign on its cusp and the planets standing in it, read against the flagship interpretation set (western-astrology-canonical) or any interpretation grammar the reader chooses. Requires a real chart calculation (birth time + place -> house cusps); data-only fallback: enter placements you already know, or read the houses as twelve standing rooms of a life. A chart is a mirror and a calendar, never a command. Rendered as a wheel by this repo's wheel.html.

casting._note: NEVER random: positions are the querent's own twelve houses. House cusps require an ephemeris-backed chart calculation (the family's engine: the Skyfield backend noted in nara's assets inventory / recursive-eco calculate-chart.py); until wired, the casting still works data-only as twelve fixed reflection rooms. Resolution: position-house-N binds to house-N of the `from` grammar, plus the derived sign-on-cusp and planets-in-house once a calc supplies them.

sections.Position: The 1st house of your chart — how you appear, begin, and enter — the mask that becomes the face. Resolved from your chart: the sign on this house's cusp and any planets standing in it.
sections.Prompt: Read this house's item in the interpretation set, then your own placements in it. What part of your life does this house name right now — and what would tending it, rather than obeying it, look like this season? The chart shows a room; you decide what happens in it.
